title: Lesson Quiz: Setting SMART Goals
description: Assessment of the concept that Specific, measurable, achievable, relevant, time-bound goals.

questions.prompt: What does the M in SMART goals stand for?

choices.feedback: Correct. The M in SMART stands for measurable.
A goal may be meaningful, but the M stands for measurable.
SMART goals can be motivating, but the M stands for measurable.
Managed is not the M in SMART.

questions.prompt: A SMART goal should include a clear time frame.

choices.feedback: SMART goals should include a time frame because they are time-bound.
Correct. A SMART goal should be time-bound.

questions.prompt: Which example best shows a specific goal?

choices.text: I want to do better someday
choices.feedback: This goal is too vague to be specific.

choices.text: I will improve my presentation score by practicing once each week
choices.feedback: This is a strong goal idea, but the clearest specific element in this set is the answer that directly states the focused action.

choices.text: I will raise my math grade by completing every assignment this quarter
choices.feedback: Correct. This goal clearly states what the student plans to do.

choices.text: I might try harder later if I remember
choices.feedback: This goal is not clear or specific.

questions.prompt: Why are SMART goals useful in planning?

choices.text: They remove the need for action steps
choices.feedback: SMART goals should support action, not remove it.

choices.text: They help students create goals that are clear and realistic
choices.feedback: This is close, but the strongest answer here is the one that directly emphasizes structure and trackability.

choices.text: They allow students to avoid deadlines
choices.feedback: SMART goals include deadlines through the time-bound part.

choices.text: They help students organize goals in a way that can be tracked and completed
choices.feedback: Correct. SMART goals make planning clearer, more organized, and easier to track.

questions.prompt: A goal can still be considered SMART even if it is not realistic.

choices.feedback: This is incorrect. A SMART goal should be achievable, which means realistic.
Correct. SMART goals should be realistic and achievable.
